commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=40081ae1ea52ee07a89f5f5481738a8ef0df7d15)
commands.review_summary:
The branch reclassifies Claude OAuth cache ownership when Claude CLI file or keychain storage is present, adds ownership regression tests, and adds an unreleased changelog entry.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection for the PR defect: the new helper returns false after `.unavailable` candidate probes, while the existing keychain presence check treats `errSecInteractionNotAllowed` as item presence. I did not run live keychain validation because AGENTS.md says unrequested real SecItem/provider probes must be avoided.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs maintainer handling because the PR still requires real setup proof and an auth-policy-sensitive keychain-presence repair that automation cannot fully validate in the contributor's environment.

target.title: Fix Claude CLI token ownership
target.body:
## Summary
- Treat cached Claude OAuth credentials as Claude CLI-owned whenever Claude CLI storage is present, even if the cache entry was previously marked CodexBar-owned.
- Detect CLI storage via the credentials file fingerprint or a no-prompt Claude Code keychain item probe.
- Keep CodexBar-owned cache entries on the direct refresh path when no Claude CLI storage is present.
- Add regression tests for CLI file ownership, delegated refresh, CodexBar-only refresh ownership, and Claude keychain ownership.

## Why
Claude Code rotates refresh tokens. If CodexBar directly refreshes a token that came from Claude CLI storage, it can desynchronize the CLI's persisted refresh token and force users to log in again. With this change, CLI-backed credentials stay delegated to Claude CLI instead of CodexBar spending the refresh token.

Fixes #1161.

## Maintainer policy
When Claude CLI storage exists, Claude Code owns the refresh lifecycle. CodexBar may read/sync the resulting credentials, but it should not independently spend the same rotating refresh token. If no Claude CLI storage is detectable, existing CodexBar-owned cache behavior is preserved.

## Validation
- `swift test --filter ClaudeOAuthCredentialsStoreCLIStorageOwnershipTests` ✅ 4 tests
- `swift test --filter ClaudeOAuthCredentialsStoreTests` ✅ 17 tests
- `swift test --filter ClaudeUsageTests` ✅ 39 tests
- `make check` ✅ SwiftFormat + SwiftLint, 0 violations
- `/Users/steipete/Projects/agent-scripts/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` ✅ clean

## Redacted behavior proof
Focused test logs show the mixed-storage path is delegated to Claude CLI, while a CodexBar-only cache still takes the direct refresh path. No real credentials were used.

```text
owner=claudeCLI source=cacheKeychain ... credentials expired; delegating refresh to Claude CLI
owner=codexbar source=cacheKeychain ... Access token expired, attempting auto-refresh
```

## Notes
- Maintainer follow-up added a negative regression test for the CodexBar-only cache path and a changelog entry crediting @RajvardhanPatil07.
target.author: rajvardhanpatil07
